us.label: US News
us.subtitle: Top domestic stories across politics, courts and immigration

articles.title: Trump set for primetime address focused on elections and voting machines
bullets: President Donald Trump scheduled a Thursday night national address from the White House.
AP reported the speech is expected to revisit election and voting-machine claims.
The address comes ahead of the 2026 midterm elections.

articles.title: DHS faces scrutiny after three fatal ICE encounters in under a week
bullets: AP reported DHS Secretary Markwayne Mullin is facing a major test over ICE tactics.
Three people died in recent encounters involving immigration officers.
The controversy follows Trump administration pressure to expand deportations.

articles.title: Trump says ICE traffic stops should continue despite suspension order
bullets: Trump posted that ICE should keep using traffic stops as an enforcement tool.
The comments followed a reported order to suspend most ICE vehicle stops.
Recent fatal shootings in Maine and Houston prompted calls for outside investigations.

articles.title: Todd Blanche faces Senate scrutiny in bid to become attorney general
bullets: Acting Attorney General Todd Blanche appeared for a Senate confirmation hearing.
Lawmakers questioned his Justice Department record and investigations of Trump critics.
Blanche previously served as Trump’s personal attorney.

articles.title: Supreme Court justices seek more security funding in rare testimony
bullets: Justices Elena Kagan and Amy Coney Barrett testified before Congress.
The court requested more funding amid rising threats against justices and judges.
The hearing also touched on ethics and court transparency issues.

global.label: Global
global.subtitle: Major worldwide developments from conflict, diplomacy, climate and sport

articles.title: US expands strikes in Iran as Hormuz confrontation escalates
bullets: AP reported U.S. strikes hit targets farther north in Iran early Thursday.
The U.S. also fired on a ship it accused of trying to break its naval blockade.
Iran warned attacks could escalate across regional infrastructure.

articles.title: Pew survey finds China viewed more favorably than US in many countries
bullets: AP reported Pew found China viewed more favorably than the U.S. in 25 of 36 surveyed places.
The poll also found Xi Jinping viewed more favorably than Trump in many countries.
Pew said it was the first such China-over-U.S. shift in roughly two decades of tracking.

articles.title: Ukraine says it downed Russian ballistic missiles while seeking more air defenses
bullets: Ukraine said it intercepted five Russian ballistic missiles in overnight attacks.
Other missiles and drones struck sites including warehouses and a school in Kyiv.
Kyiv is seeking stronger air defenses before winter attacks on energy infrastructure.

articles.title: More than 500 Rohingya feared dead after reported boat capsizings
bullets: UN agencies said two boats carrying Rohingya refugees may have capsized in the Bay of Bengal.
One boat reportedly carried about 250 people and another about 280.
Officials said casualty figures were still being verified.

articles.title: Argentina reaches World Cup final against Spain after beating England
bullets: Argentina rallied late to beat England 2-1 in the World Cup semifinal.
Enzo Fernandez and Lautaro Martinez scored after England led 1-0.
Argentina will face Spain in Sunday’s final in East Rutherford, New Jersey.

india.label: India News
india.subtitle: Top stories from India politics, security, economy and space

articles.title: CRPF deploys 100 bulletproof vehicles after Manipur tensions
bullets: CRPF revised troop movement and field-deployment protocols in Manipur.
Officials said about 100 bulletproof vehicles were sent to strengthen force protection.
The changes followed tensions and an attack on an Assam Rifles camp in Senapati.

articles.title: Congress urges Sonam Wangchuk to end hunger strike
bullets: Congress appealed to activist Sonam Wangchuk to end his fast at Jantar Mantar.
The party said it shared protesters’ anger over alleged exam paper leaks.
Wangchuk’s protest has demanded the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan.

articles.title: One devotee dies and over 30 hospitalised after Puri Rath Yatra crowd rush
bullets: A devotee reportedly died of suffocation during the Rath Yatra in Puri.
More than 30 people were taken to hospital after the crowd rush.
Officials said several devotees were rescued after falling during the procession.

articles.title: Skyroot to launch Vikram-1, India’s first private orbital rocket, on July 18
bullets: Skyroot Aerospace plans to launch Vikram-1 from Sriharikota on July 18.
The mission would mark India’s first privately developed orbital rocket launch.
The launch is seen as a milestone for India’s commercial space sector.

articles.title: India tells shipping firms not to deploy Indian sailors through Hormuz
bullets: India’s shipping regulator warned companies against deploying Indian seafarers through the Strait of Hormuz.
The advisory follows escalating attacks and military activity in the waterway.
Indian officials have reported casualties and missing personnel linked to Gulf-region attacks.

articles.title: SpaceX shares dip below IPO price as lockup expiry nears
bullets: Reuters reported SpaceX briefly fell below its $135 IPO price on Wednesday.
The stock has dropped sharply from its post-IPO high.
Investors are watching upcoming lockup releases and the company’s first public earnings report.

articles.title: AI financing boom lifts Wall Street deal expectations
bullets: Reuters reported banks are seeing AI infrastructure drive capital raising and deal activity.
Goldman Sachs cited a multiyear AI investment cycle during earnings commentary.
The trend is affecting financing, IPO and strategic advisory pipelines.

articles.title: TSMC’s Q2 earnings call puts chip and AI demand in focus
bullets: TSMC scheduled its second-quarter 2026 earnings conference for July 16.
The company’s guidance pointed to higher expected Q2 revenue versus Q1 actual revenue.
Investors are watching TSMC for signals on AI chip demand and semiconductor capacity.

articles.title: SK Hynix remains a key AI-memory mover after volatile US debut
bullets: Yahoo Finance reported SK Hynix’s Nasdaq debut tested investor appetite for AI memory stocks.
Shares opened above the IPO price during the July listing session.
The stock remains closely tied to AI server and high-bandwidth memory demand.

articles.title: IBM weakness highlights split between AI infrastructure and legacy tech
bullets: Market updates cited IBM as a large-cap laggard after earnings-related concerns.
The move contrasted with stronger investor interest in AI infrastructure names.
Traders are distinguishing between direct AI beneficiaries and slower-growth enterprise technology firms.

business.label: Business
business.subtitle: Corporate, finance and trade stories moving boardrooms

articles.title: Wall Street banks say AI buildout is boosting deals and financing
bullets: Reuters reported AI infrastructure spending is creating more financing work for banks.
Goldman Sachs said the AI buildout remains in its early stages.
Bankers expect the cycle to support capital raising, loans and M&A activity.

articles.title: SpaceX selloff raises investor focus on post-IPO lockups
bullets: SpaceX shares have fallen near or below the company’s IPO price.
Reuters said employee and early-investor share-sale restrictions will begin lifting after its first quarterly report.
The company remains one of Wall Street’s largest by market value despite the decline.

articles.title: India-UK trade deal takes effect as Modi highlights MSME benefits
bullets: PM Narendra Modi called the India-UK agreement a significant moment in the partnership.
The deal is expected to deepen trade links across goods, services and technology.
Modi said farmers, entrepreneurs and MSMEs would benefit.

articles.title: India approves new urea investment policy to boost domestic production
bullets: The Cabinet approved the National Investment Policy for Urea-2026.
The policy is designed to encourage investment in gas-based urea manufacturing.
Officials said it aims to reduce import dependence and strengthen fertilizer supply.

articles.title: TSMC earnings event draws investor attention to semiconductor margins
bullets: TSMC held its second-quarter 2026 earnings conference on July 16 in Taipei.
Its published guidance showed expected revenue of $39.0 billion to $40.2 billion for Q2.
Gross-margin and operating-margin guidance were also higher than the prior quarter’s guidance range.

tech.label: Tech
tech.subtitle: AI, chips, space and emerging-technology developments

articles.title: Skyroot prepares Vikram-1 private orbital launch from Sriharikota
bullets: Skyroot Aerospace plans a July 18 launch for Vikram-1.
The mission would be India’s first privately developed orbital rocket launch.
The launch could expand India’s private space-technology ecosystem.

articles.title: SpaceX readies first Starship test flight since going public
bullets: ABC News reported SpaceX’s first Starship test flight as a public company is set to launch.
The mission comes after SpaceX’s historic IPO and volatile stock trading.
Any mission issues could affect both Starship timelines and investor sentiment.

articles.title: TSMC earnings spotlight AI chip demand and capacity plans
bullets: TSMC scheduled its Q2 2026 earnings call for July 16.
Investors are watching revenue, margins and capacity signals tied to AI demand.
The company is a critical supplier for advanced semiconductors used in AI systems.

articles.title: UN panel releases preliminary report on global AI governance
bullets: A UN scientific panel published a preliminary AI report in July 2026.
The report reviews AI company revenue trends and global governance issues.
It adds to international scrutiny of AI safety, competition and economic effects.

articles.title: AI infrastructure spending creates a Wall Street financing boom
bullets: Reuters reported tech companies’ AI buildout is driving financing demand.
Banks expect elevated strategic activity and capital formation tied to AI infrastructure.
The trend affects cloud, chips, data centers and public-market listings.
